label: Operation Serval — Barkhane Axis (Bamako → Gao)
notes: French Serval advance northward from Bamako through Sévaré and Gao; main French counter-offensive axis Jan–Feb 2013.

label: Operation Serval — Western Axis (Bamako → Timbuktu)
notes: French Serval advance along the western axis through Niono and Léré toward Timbuktu; city liberated January 27, 2013.

label: Operation Serval — Gao to Kidal Advance
notes: French special forces advance from Gao to secure Kidal on January 30, 2013. Chadian forces reinforced the position.

label: Jihadist Advance — Gao to Konna (Jan 2013)
notes: AQIM/Ansar Dine/MUJAO advance from Gao southward to Konna on January 10, 2013 — the move that triggered French intervention.

label: Jihadist Second Axis — Gao to Diabaly (Jan 2013)
notes: MUJAO second axis of advance capturing Diabaly on January 14, 2013, threatening the Ségou-Bamako corridor before French airstrikes halted the advance.

label: JNIM Timbuktu Blockade Corridor
notes: JNIM supply-interdiction corridor from Mopti Region northward along the Niger River, cutting road access to Timbuktu since 2023.

label: Katiba Macina Expansion — Mopti to Ségou
notes: Katiba Macina expansion corridor from its Mopti heartland westward into Ségou Region 2015–2019, exploiting Fulani communal grievances.

label: JNIM Western Expansion — Ségou to Kayes (→ Senegal)
notes: JNIM westward expansion from Ségou through Kayes Region toward Mauritania and Senegal borders 2023–2025; multiple attacks on gendarmerie posts.

label: ISGS Expansion into Liptako-Gourma Tri-Border Zone
notes: ISGS expansion from Ménaka base southward into the Liptako-Gourma tri-border zone 2017–2020, establishing the 'triangle of death'.

label: IS-Sahel Expansion into Northern Burkina Faso
notes: IS-Sahel Province expansion southward from the tri-border zone into Burkina Faso's Sahel Region (Dori, Djibo corridor) from 2019.

label: IS-Sahel Attacks in Tillabéri (Niger)
notes: ISGS/IS-Sahel attack corridor from Ménaka eastward into Niger's Tillabéri Region; multiple major base attacks including Tongo Tongo (2017) and Inates (2019).

label: Wagner/Africa Corps Deployment — Bamako to Mopti
notes: Initial Wagner Group deployment from Bamako to Mopti Region in December 2021, the first operational zone after arrival.

label: Wagner/Africa Corps Northward — Mopti to Gao
notes: Wagner Group extending operations northward from Mopti to Gao from early 2022, taking over former French Barkhane operational zones.

label: FAMa/Africa Corps Kidal Offensive Column (Oct–Nov 2023)
notes: FAMa-Wagner column of ~115 vehicles departing Gao October 2, 2023 and advancing to Kidal, capturing the city November 14, 2023.

label: Africa Corps Deployment — Gao to Timbuktu
notes: Africa Corps deployment to Timbuktu airbase after Barkhane withdrawal from Mali. Russian personnel base struck by JNIM June 2, 2025.

label: JNIM Attack Axis — Mopti to Kati (Sept 2024)
notes: JNIM attack column approach route toward Kati garrison near Bamako, September 17, 2024 — first direct attack on the capital area in the conflict.

label: Operation Barkhane Withdrawal — Gao to Niamey (Aug 2022)
notes: Last French Barkhane soldiers cross from Mali into Niger on August 15, 2022, following expulsion by the Malian junta.

label: JNIM Attack — Boulkessi Base (Jun 2025)
notes: JNIM assault route toward Boulkessi military base near the Mali-Burkina Faso border, June 1, 2025; 60+ killed, 220 taken hostage.

label: JNIM Coordinated Assault — Timbuktu (Jun 2025)
notes: JNIM multi-point coordinated assault on Timbuktu including the Russian Africa Corps airbase, June 2, 2025; 60+ FAMa killed.

label: MNLA Advance — Kidal to Aguelhok (Jan 2012)
notes: MNLA initial offensive axis capturing Aguelhok January 17–24, 2012; site of massacre of ~82 Malian soldiers.

label: Rebel Sweep — Kidal to Gao (Mar 2012)
notes: MNLA and MUJAO sweep from the Kidal-Tessalit axis southward to capture Gao on March 31, 2012, exploiting the political vacuum after the coup.

label: JNIM Cross-Border Expansion — Mali to Burkina Faso
notes: JNIM/Katiba Macina expansion from Mopti Region southward across the Mali-Burkina Faso border 2016–2020, establishing logistics and recruitment networks in northern Burkina Faso.

label: JNIM Expansion — Burkina Faso East toward Niger
notes: JNIM/IS-Sahel eastward expansion from Burkina Faso's Est Region toward Niger's Dosso and Tillabéri border areas from 2019.

label: JNIM Southward Expansion — Burkina to Benin/W Park
notes: JNIM/IS-Sahel southward expansion from Fada N'Gourma toward the W National Park zone and Benin/Togo border areas 2021–2025.

label: ISGS Ambush — Ménaka to Tongo Tongo (Oct 2017)
notes: ISGS attack trajectory for the October 4, 2017 ambush near Tongo Tongo, Niger that killed 4 US Special Forces.

label: France Withdrawal — Niamey to N'Djamena (2023)
notes: France's phased withdrawal of 1,500 troops from Niger to N'Djamena, Chad following the Niger junta's expulsion demand in 2023.

label: FAMa/Africa Corps Supply Axis — Gao to Tessalit
notes: FAMa and Africa Corps logistical supply line from Gao northward to Tessalit following MINUSMA withdrawal and Kidal capture.

label: JNIM Convoy Ambush Corridor — Gao-Bourem Road
notes: JNIM repeated ambush corridor on the Gao-Bourem road and surrounding areas 2022–2024; FAMa convoys regularly targeted.

label: JNIM Siege Corridor — Ouagadougou to Djibo
notes: JNIM blockade of road access from Ouagadougou northward toward Djibo, forcing the Sahel Region provincial capital to rely entirely on costly airlifts for resupply.

label: JNIM Blockade — Kaya to Dori Road
notes: JNIM interdiction of the Kaya-Dori road — the main supply artery to Burkina Faso's Sahel Region — repeatedly ambushed from 2022, accelerating humanitarian collapse.
